title: A Brand New Game - Lyrics Guru

content.text: Lyrics Guru is a card-based game designed to help with memory and cognitive engagement in elderly players. The game uses popular song lyrics from a wide variety of genres and artists, asking players to fill in the missing words. Lyrics Guru needed a design system for its cards and packaging that would be intuitive and accessible. The visuals had to clearly represent the artist, genre, or song while remaining simple and easy to understand, even for players with memory challenges. The goal was to create a fun, engaging, and approachable game for all ages.

Rule27 Design developed a design that balances clarity, accessibility, and playful energy. Each card features clear typography, color coding, and visual cues that indicate the genre or artist, making it easy for players to recognize and engage with the content. The packaging design was created to be welcoming and intuitive, with straightforward instructions and bold imagery that invites interaction. Every element was designed to support memory retention while keeping the experience fun and visually appealing.

meta_title: A Brand New Game - Lyrics Guru
meta_description: Creating a brand new game isn't easy, creating a branded look and feel for the game is even harder.

implementation_process.html: We created a complete visual system for the cards, packaging, and promotional materials. Typography, color, and imagery were carefully considered to ensure legibility and comprehension. The result is a design that allows players to focus on the game and enjoy the experience without confusion or frustration. Lyrics Guru now has a polished, approachable identity that enhances the gameplay experience and makes it accessible to a wide audience.

results_summary: Created fun, engaging and memorable spaces for the spinning wheel that selects the category from which a player must answer a question.

title: Launching GoodFinch Photography

content.text: GoodFinch Photography is a Phoenix-based studio known for capturing moments that feel authentic, warm, and full of life. They wanted to expand their reach by creating a cohesive brand identity that reflected the quality and artistry of their work. The goal was to build brand awareness, drive traffic to their social channels, and attract new clients. GoodFinch needed a visual system that could translate the beauty of their photography into an online presence that felt consistent, professional, and unmistakably their own.

Rule27 Design created a brand identity that put GoodFinch’s artistry at the center. We developed a color palette inspired by natural light and texture, paired with elegant typography that felt timeless but approachable. Every visual element was designed to complement the photography rather than compete with it. The new look captures the warmth and sophistication of the brand while giving GoodFinch a recognizable and consistent voice across social media and marketing materials.

meta_description: How we branded a local photographers side hustle.

content.text: We provided a full brand package, including logo variations, color systems, and font pairings that could adapt across platforms. From website headers to Instagram posts, the refreshed brand brought cohesion and polish to every touchpoint. The result is an identity that feels as intentional and expressive as the photography itself, helping GoodFinch stand out in a crowded creative market and connect more deeply with clients who appreciate the art behind every image.

results_summary: Helped a local photographer launch a brand for her side hustle.

title: Rebranding Sunnyside Care Home

content.text: Sunnyside Care Home has always been known for its compassion and dedication to residents, but their brand didn’t reflect the warmth people felt once they walked through the doors. The logo and color palette were dated, and the overall tone didn’t convey the trust and optimism families were looking for when choosing care for their loved ones. Sunnyside wanted to brighten things up, both visually and emotionally, and position themselves as professional yet approachable. The goal was simple: create a brand that matched the heart of their care and helped attract new families to their community.

At Rule27 Design, we set out to reimagine Sunnyside’s identity from the ground up. We created a new logo that felt fresh but familiar, paired with a friendly typeface and a bright, uplifting color palette inspired by the Arizona sun. Every element was chosen to communicate warmth, optimism, and trust. Beyond visuals, we refined the brand voice to sound more conversational and welcoming, professional but never clinical. The new identity gave Sunnyside a way to express who they truly are: A place where care feels personal and genuine.

meta_description: How we increased resident applications by 21%

content.text: Once the refreshed brand was ready, we helped Sunnyside bring it to life across every touchpoint including signage, uniforms, print materials, and digital platforms. The transformation was immediate. Staff members embraced the new look with enthusiasm, saying it made them feel prouder and more connected to their mission. Families responded to the warmth and clarity of the new brand, and the care home saw a noticeable increase in new inquiries.

The Sunnyside rebrand became more than just a visual update; it became a reflection of their purpose. Bright, trustworthy, and human at its core, the new identity helped everyone who encountered Sunnyside feel what residents have always known: this is a place built on care.

results_summary: Revolutionized patient care delivery through innovative digital solutions, streamlined workflows, and user-centered design that improved outcomes while reducing operational costs.

title: Kicks, Cash and Creativity

content.text: Ball Till U Fall is a sneaker reselling business built on passion, style, and community. Mr. Clarke, the owner and operator, had established a strong local presence but knew his brand needed a fresh look to grow. He wanted to attract new customers, build trust, and communicate that his business offered only the finest quality sneakers. The goal was to create an identity that felt authentic and approachable while projecting the credibility and polish of a premium reseller.

Rule27 Design partnered with Mr. Clarke to develop a brand that matched his dedication and drive. We designed a new logo that felt bold and confident, paired with a visual system that balanced streetwear culture with modern sophistication. The branding communicates trust and energy, connecting with sneaker enthusiasts who value both authenticity and quality. Every detail was chosen to make Ball Till U Fall instantly recognizable, from the logo design to the color palette and typography.

meta_title: Kicks, Cash and Creativity - The Ball Till U Fall Story
meta_description: Ball Till U Fall is a sneaker reselling business built on passion, style, and community.

content.text: Once the new identity was complete, we extended the design across business cards, social media templates, and digital assets that would help Mr. Clarke show up consistently online and at in-person events. With his refreshed look, he entered buy, sell, and trade shows with renewed confidence and a brand that matched the quality of the sneakers he sold.
Ball Till U Fall now stands out as a trustworthy, stylish, and professional reseller brand that reflects both the passion of its founder and the culture of the sneaker community he serves.

results_summary: Transformed a struggling tech startup into a market leader through comprehensive brand strategy, cutting-edge web development, and data-driven marketing campaigns that delivered unprecedented growth.

title: How NMHL Grew Organic Visibility 92x in 7 Months

content.text: NMHL was operating in one of the most competitive organic search markets in the country — mortgage and lending. Despite offering strong rates and local expertise, their website was generating fewer than 80 impressions per day in Google. Competitors with larger content teams and domain authority were capturing nearly all organic search traffic for the queries NMHL's customers were making daily. The site had no programmatic content strategy and zero presence for the local and state-specific mortgage search terms their customers were actually using.

Rule27 deployed a full OLG engagement — researching every mortgage-related query in NMHL's market, validating targets against actual Google SERPs, and deploying 7,000+ SEO-optimized pages across loan types, locations, rates, and buyer scenarios. Every page was manually submitted to Google Search Console for indexing. The result was a systematic capture of long-tail mortgage search traffic that national brands were not competing for.

deliverables: Custom Next.js website on Rule27 infrastructure
Google Search Console setup and daily indexing
meta_title: NMHL Case Study: 92x Organic Growth in 7 Months | Rule27 Design
meta_description: How Rule27 deployed 7,000+ SEO pages for NMHL and grew daily impressions from 79 to 7,300+ — a 92x increase in organic visibility over 7 months.
og_description: Rule27 deployed 7,000+ targeted SEO pages for NMHL. Daily impressions grew 92x in 7 months — all organic, no ad spend.

content.text: We started with a full competitor and keyword gap analysis across national and regional mortgage lenders, identifying thousands of search queries NMHL had no presence for. After SERP validation on every priority target, we designed the URL architecture across loan types, state pages, city pages, rate pages, and borrower scenario pages. 7,000+ pages were generated with NMHL-specific context, reviewed against our quality gate, and deployed with daily manual Google Search Console submission. The first impression spike appeared within 7 days of deployment. By month 7, daily impressions had grown from 79 to 7,300+ — a 92x increase in organic visibility.

results_summary: 79 daily impressions to 7,300+ daily impressions — a 92x increase in organic visibility over 7 months with 7,000+ pages deployed and indexed.

gallery_images.caption: Google Search Console — 1 week after deployment
Real-time visitors before deployment — 32 in 30 minutes
Real-time visitors after deployment — 2,836 in 30 minutes

title: How FreedomDev Grew Organic Visibility 99x in 12 Months

content.text: FreedomDev is a custom software development firm competing in a crowded market. Despite being operationally strong, their website had virtually no organic search presence — just 39 daily impressions at project start. Competitors with established domain authority were capturing all organic traffic for the enterprise software development queries FreedomDev was best positioned to win.

Rule27 deployed 1,000+ targeted pages covering FreedomDev's industry verticals, technology specializations, and geographic service areas. The deployment grew daily impressions from 39 to 3,876 — a 99x increase over 12 months. Phase 2 of the engagement focuses on CTR optimization, rewriting title tags and meta descriptions on high-impression pages to convert visibility into clicks and leads.

deliverables: Custom Next.js website on Rule27 infrastructure
meta_title: FreedomDev Case Study: 99x Organic Growth in 12 Months | Rule27 Design
meta_description: How Rule27 deployed 1,000+ SEO pages for FreedomDev and grew daily impressions from 39 to 3,876 — a 99x increase in organic visibility over 12 months.
og_description: Rule27 deployed 1,000+ targeted SEO pages for FreedomDev, growing organic visibility 99x over 12 months.
internal_notes: DO NOT PUBLISH until CTR optimization shows measurable click improvement. Current CTR is 0.08% — pages are in Google but not winning clicks. Three options: (1) Publish with honest Phase 1 visibility framing, (2) Hold until Phase 2 click data exists, (3) Publish as before/after optimization story once CTR improves. Josh to decide.

content.text: We mapped FreedomDev's target industries, technology stack, and geographic service areas against competitor keyword data, then designed a URL taxonomy across industry pages, technology pages, solution pages, and service-location clusters. 1,000+ pages were deployed with manual GSC submission, growing impressions 99x over 12 months. The current phase involves title and meta description rewrites targeting the 25+ pages ranking position 5–20 with high impressions and low CTR — converting accumulated visibility into qualified traffic.

results_summary: 39 daily impressions to 3,876 daily impressions — a 99x increase in organic visibility over 12 months. 1,000+ pages deployed and indexed. CTR optimization phase active.
